issues: The draft describes Steve Jaques as a former 'Tri-City Americans defenseman and captain' without mentioning his Sockeyes connection — the OCR refers to him as 'Spenner and Steve Jaques' gaining celebrity as the 'Bruise Brothers,' indicating Jaques' earlier career included the Sockeyes (Stan Czenczek, aka 'Spenner/Stemmer', is a known Sockeyes player). The article is a broader multi-player follow-up column, not solely about Jaques.

The draft headline 'Steve Jaques' is correct for the Jaques section but the OCR is a multi-subject column covering several ex-Americans players (Krauss, LeBrun, Kolzig, Jaques, etc.); the actual headline/column title is not cleanly visible in the OCR but the Jaques section header appears as '• STEVE JAQUES •'.
The draft short description omits the Sockeyes connection (Jaques and Stan Czenczek aka 'Spenner' were known as the 'Bruise Brothers,' linking this article to the 1987 Sockeyes roster).
review.correctedDescriptionShort: A multi-player follow-up column on ex-Tri-City Americans, including a section on former Sockeyes defenseman Steve Jaques — who with Sockeye Stan Czenczek ("Spenner") formed the "Bruise Brothers" — covering his 1990-91 season with the IHL's Phoenix Roadrunners.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A Tri-City Herald column traces the post-junior careers of several former Americans players, including a dedicated section on Steve Jaques, a defenseman who played for the 1987 Richmond Sockeyes alongside Stan Czenczek ("Spenner") as the famed "Bruise Brothers." Jaques served as captain of the Tri-City Americans during the 1989-90 WHL season before spending 1990-91 with the Phoenix Roadrunners of the IHL, a Los Angeles Kings farm team. In 58 regular-season games he recorded 10 goals, 24 assists, and 227 penalty minutes; in nine playoff games he added 2 goals, 6 assists, and 21 penalty minutes before Phoenix was eliminated by Peoria in the Turner Cup semifinals. A Roadrunners broadcaster noted Jaques "grew more aggressive" and finished the year at plus-7.
review.notes: The OCR covers a multi-subject career-update column; the Steve Jaques section is the Sockeyes-relevant portion. The lede explicitly links Jaques with Stan Czenczek ("Spenner") as the "Bruise Brothers," confirming Sockeyes relevance. The column also mentions Olie Kolzig (ex-Portland, not a Sockeye) and other ex-Americans. No clear standalone headline or byline is recoverable from the OCR for this column.
